title: Delicious Brazilian Chicken Tagine
description: A malty, juicy, and energizing Brazilian dish featuring cashew nuts, cooked in a traditional tagine style, whole30-friendly and served in a Korean brass bowl.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in the Korean brass bowl over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken breast and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add the chopped cashew nuts, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Pour in 1 cup of water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the bowl, and simmer for 25 minutes.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
